title: Tool Execution (Approved Tools)
description: Dispatching and executing approved tool calls after HITL resume: resolving approval status, dispatching function/custom tools, and gathering results.
interaction_flow: Caller → execute_approved_tools(agent, interruptions, context_wrapper, generated_items, run_config, hooks, all_tools)
execute_approved_tools: build tool_map from all_tools (FunctionTool + named non-FunctionTool)
_resolve_tool_run: if approval_status is False → resolve_approval_rejection_message() → _append_error() → return None
_resolve_tool_run: if approval_status is not True → _append_error('unclear') → return None
_resolve_tool_run: if tool not found or not FunctionTool → _append_error() → return None
execute_approved_tools → execute_function_tool_calls(bindings, tool_runs, hooks, context_wrapper, config)
_maybe_execute_tool_approval: if approval_status is False → resolve_approval_rejection_message() → return FunctionToolResult(rejection)
_FunctionToolBatchExecutor → _build_function_tool_results(): for each tool_run consume nested agent run results / interruptions
execute_custom_tool_calls: for each ToolRunCustom → CustomToolAction.execute(agent, call, hooks, context_wrapper, config)
resolve_approval_status: if status None and on_approval callback → call on_approval(context_wrapper, approval_item) → context_wrapper.approve_tool/reject_tool
CustomToolAction.execute: if rejected → resolve_approval_rejection_message() → return rejection ToolCallOutputItem
CustomToolAction.execute: if status not True → return ToolApprovalItem (interrupt)
key_relationships: execute_approved_tools calls execute_function_tool_calls for all approved FunctionTool interruptions
_FunctionToolBatchExecutor spawns asyncio.Task per tool via _create_tool_task; waits with _drain_pending_tasks
_run_single_tool calls _maybe_execute_tool_approval first; if not blocked calls _execute_single_tool_body
_execute_single_tool_body runs input guardrails, fires on_tool_start hooks, then spawns _invoke_tool_and_run_post_invoke task
_invoke_tool_and_run_post_invoke runs the actual tool, output guardrails, and on_tool_end hooks
resolve_approval_status is shared util: builds ToolApprovalItem, checks context_wrapper approval status, optionally calls on_approval callback
resolve_approval_rejection_message checks per-call rejection_message override first, then run_config.tool_error_formatter, then default REJECTION_MESSAGE
execute_custom_tool_calls iterates serially; CustomToolAction.execute handles approval check internally via resolve_approval_status
_settle_pending_function_tool_tasks is the bounded timeout loop: calls _collect_settled_function_tool_tasks repeatedly until deadline or done

approval_states.None: No decision stored → create ToolApprovalItem and surface as interruption
approval_states.False: Rejected → resolve rejection message and return error item to model

parallel_execution_notes: FunctionTool calls execute in parallel via asyncio.Task per call. isolate_parallel_failures=True (default when >1 call) cancels siblings on first failure. _settle_pending_function_tool_tasks drains with a timeout bound. CustomTool and Shell calls execute serially.
key_types: ToolApprovalItem: (agent, raw_item, tool_name, tool_namespace, tool_origin, tool_lookup_key)
ToolCallOutputItem: wraps model-visible output + raw API item
important_notes: execute_approved_tools is the HITL resume path — called after RunState.approve() to re-execute previously interrupted tool calls. It only handles FunctionTool calls (non-function tools like ShellTool have separate approval handling in their own action classes). The _settle_pending_function_tool_tasks function is an internal helper for the FunctionToolBatchExecutor, not called from execute_approved_tools directly.
